reasoning: The base rate of any MEP (out of 720) resigning in a random 3-week window is roughly 15-20%, given typical turnover due to health, national politics, or other reasons. The presence of an active "Huawei lobbying scandal" (as indicated by the background) increases the likelihood of a resignation. However, recent history (like Qatargate) shows that MEPs implicated in scandals often refuse to resign immediately, instead losing their committee or parliament roles while waiting out legal processes. Balancing the increased pressure of a new scandal against this historical reluctance to resign quickly, I estimate the probability of at least one resignation in this specific 3-week window (June 9 - July 1) to be around 33%.
